headline.summary: Highest LPEA PSPS concern is Mon, Jun 1 near Pagosa Springs (LIKELY 97/100), driven by red-flag wind/gust signal near 25 mph; very dry RH near 10%; 3 sampled hours meet red-flag screen.
headline.trend_summary: Momentum is steady versus the prior run (May 30 at 5:21 AM MDT); forecast volatility is high and first WATCH-or-higher date is Sun, May 31.

method_notes: Rules-based decision support using public weather, fire-posture, and LPEA source signals.
Scores are screening estimates, not statistically calibrated probabilities or official forecasts.
LPEA may use internal circuit, asset, crew, outage, and operational data this monitor cannot see.
notable_changes: First WATCH-or-higher PSPS date moved later from Sat, May 30 to Sun, May 31.
Fri, Jun 5: worsening vs prior run; PSPS ELEVATED -> WATCH; score +4, wind +1 mph, RH 0%, red-flag hours 0.
Thu, Jun 4: easing vs prior run; PSPS ELEVATED -> ELEVATED; score -14, wind 0 mph, RH 0%, red-flag hours 0. Driver shifted to Ignacio / southeast La Plata County.

scope: Public-source screening analysis for weather-driven Red Flag and LPEA PSPS risk.

trend.first_watch_shift: First WATCH-or-higher PSPS date moved later from Sat, May 30 to Sun, May 31.

unofficial_notice: This is not an official forecast, National Weather Service warning, LPEA outage notice, or LPEA Public Safety Power Shutoff notice. Confirm conditions and safety decisions with NWS and LPEA.
watch_next: Check whether the largest day-level changes line up with wind/RH movement or public-source context.
Check whether the LPEA active match is still a broad red-flag banner rather than direct PSPS/outage intent.
Watch whether the highest-risk locations remain consistent across runs or the driver area is moving.
If a PSPS occurs, log the confirmed date, location, and source so future hit-rate scoring can improve.
